evidence.authorization: Rewrite and close documentation and plan assets only; do not migrate implementation code; use Fireworks Tech Graph for consistent architecture diagrams; stop for user audit after completion
confirmed_decisions: D38-D48: AI host LLM is the primary semantic finding producer; Python prepares mechanical structure, validates anchors, and renders; deterministic rules are deferred

D49-D59: ReviewPack and ReviewResult are internal versioned transports; finalize owns ingest and graph assembly; render remains a pure audit.json consumer; intermediate artifacts are diagnosable but not user-facing
D60-D70: merge CrossReview into change_audit.review; one product, one package, one Skill; preserve review tests and eval behavior

D71-D90: baseline-first migration, isolated eval data, prompt-lab harness migration, no PyPI publish in v0, change-audit remains the product name, old crossreview CLI is not shipped in the new distribution
D91: review is a Skill user action, not a Python command; Python exposes prepare/finalize/render
D92: hidden .run transport directory; clean on success, retain on failure or explicit keep flag
D93 revised: review_status describes execution; verdict describes review conclusion; no no_findings verdict
D94: exact anchor required for bug; file-only allowed for risk/quality/scope; adapter maps categories and computes risk independently of CrossReview quality_metrics
D95: summary.review_status is independent with not_reviewed/complete/partial/failed
D96: an unanchored semantic bug is retained as a downgraded risk with original category and downgrade reason

D97: only validated anchored findings enter the provisional 0-100 score; downgraded unanchored risks are counted separately; if they are the only risks, risk_score is null and verdict is needs_human_triage; weights freeze after Wave 2 dogfood

provenance.note: Consolidates previously discussed decisions that were not yet represented in machine receipts
